items.title: In Barrow Creek a $5 note hangs on the pub's wall, still waiting for Peter Falconio

children.content: Twenty-five years ago, the murder of British backpacker Peter Falconio and the abduction and escape of his girlfriend Joanne Lees in the remote Central Australian outback captured global attention. Today, it remains one of the nation's most infamous cases.

articles.title: Police release video of killer Bradley Murdoch filmed weeks before his death

children.content: In never-before-seen footage of police questioning Bradley John Murdoch in his final weeks, the notorious murderer repeatedly tells officers he "knows nothing" about the location of British tourist Peter Falconio's remains.

image.altText: Body-worn vision from a police officer shows a tall, older man speaking to two people and pointing his finger.

gallery.altText: A old photo of couple sitting in the front seats of a Kombi van, they are turned around to look at the camera.
An Australian five dollar note stuck to a wall with other notes and papers reading PETER + PAUL FALCONIO
A woman sitting at the bar inside a dim hotel, looking over the mementos plastered on the walls around her.

thumbnail.altText: A old photo of couple sitting in the front seats of a Kombi van, they are turned around to look at the camera.
An Australian five dollar note stuck to a wall with other notes and papers reading PETER + PAUL FALCONIO

items.title: Violence against women under national spotlight after four deaths in four days

children.content: Experts are calling for more research and better funding for domestic violence prevention after four women and girls were killed in separate incidents last week.

image.altText: A composite showing photos of three women, and a fourth picture that shows the shadow outline of a girl

items.title: Why are the world's biggest AI firms so attracted to the NT?

children.content: The NT's sunny skies, natural gas reserves and proximity to Asia have caught the attention of a dozen data centre developers.

image.altText: A computer-generated image of a huge industrial site in an outback area.

items.title: Shock resignation of NT children's commissioner deemed 'a tragedy'

children.content: Australia's peak advocate for Aboriginal children has described the circumstances around child protection in the Northern Territory as "dire", following the shock resignation of the Territory's children's commissioner.

articles.title: Children's commissioner quits as CLP set to pass controversial welfare changes

children.content: Shahleena Musk's resignation comes as the Northern Territory government-dominated scrutiny committee backs the Country Liberal Party's changes to child protection laws, despite widespread opposition and confusion about a key aspect of the legislation.

image.altText: A woman wearing a black dress looking directly at the camera.

items.title: NT government facing Federal Court over 'draconian' compensation cap laws

children.content: The NT government's controversial laws capping compensation for prisoners unlawfully assaulted, strip searched or imprisoned are being challenged in the Federal Court.

image.altText: A building painted in black and brown reads: NAAJA.

items.title: Man's death 'not suspicious' after 'large fight' on APY Lands, police say

children.content: Police said the death of a 59-year-old man was not suspicious after a fight broke out on South Australia's APY Lands, but investigations into the disturbance are continuing.

image.altText: A drone of houses with mountain range behind them.

items.title: Crocs, sharks and an Aussie cricket legend: All the action from the Barra Classic

children.content: Crocs, sharks and an Australian cricket legend have all turned up for one of Australia's biggest barra fishing tournaments on the Daly River.

items.title: 'Jurassic family': Spielberg leads outpouring of tributes for actor Sam Neill

children.content: The New Zealand actor died suddenly at the age of 78, with Hollywood directors and actors and prominent political figures all paying tribute to a great of global cinema.

items.title: Self-managed super funds could have to pay compensation

items.title: Video appears to show Thailand fire 'flashover' event, expert says

children.content: A video analysis of an explosive pub fire in Thailand, which killed 27 people, appears to show a flashover "point of no return" that would have made evacuation nearly impossible, an expert says.

items.title: 'Ovulating, come over now': The seedy side of online sperm donation

children.content: Australia is facing a sperm donation shortage and it is leading those desperate to find a way to conceive down some expensive and potentially dangerous paths.

items.title: Ukraine and allies join to protect Europe from ballistic missiles

items.title: Yemen's Houthis target Saudi airport with ballistic missiles and drones

items.title: 'Gatekeeping septuagenarians': 'Inappropriate' TikTok video deleted

children.content: The National Trust of SA says one of its volunteers apologised after posting a social media clip labelling a group of disaffected members "gatekeeping septuagenarians" who were running a "stuffy museum".

image.altText: "Sex is great... but have you ever experienced the euphoria of ripping down every single no photo sign in a stuffy museum?"
